tasks_completed.description: Iteration 2: Remove inline bash invocation from commands/clean.md line 589; replace with slash-command-only direction (/commit then /push)
tasks_completed.changes: Line 589: removed 'run bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly' (inline bash invocation violating AC1). New text: 'If satisfied: Changes already committed — to publish to remote: run `/commit` (to register the commit and write the push token), then `/push`'
tasks_completed.rationale: QA iteration 1 rejection: line 589 included an inline bash invocation ('bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly') as primary workaround, violating BA spec AC1 requirement for 'direction toward next command (slash command), not inline git invocations'. The /commit then /push slash-command chain is the correct resolution per orchestrator's accepted end-state.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — architectural gap, not a regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: clean.md was authored with a different architecture (cleaner commits inline, no dev-cycle artifact chain). The /push ending-workflow pattern from dev.md was never propagated to clean.md or other file-modifying commands outside the dev-cycle architecture.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Adds the missing next-step direction to /push in Step 17's Next Steps block, using slash commands only (/commit then /push). The push-gate token constraint is acknowledged via the parenthetical '(to register the commit and write the push token)' — explaining why /commit must precede /push — without resorting to inline bash invocations.

dev.qa_notes: Verify: (1) No bare bash/shell invocations in commands/clean.md Step 17 Next Steps block. (2) /push appears in clean.md ~line 589. (3) /close does NOT appear as a required step in that block. (4) Both satisfied (line 589) and unsatisfied (line 590) branches are present. (5) agents/cleaner.md is unchanged.

blast_radius.impact: cross-reference/index files only; no functional callers broken

self_verification.notes: Verified via Read of commands/clean.md lines 586-592: line 589 contains '/commit' and '/push' only, no bare bash invocation. Pre-existing guards at lines 468 and 479 (checkpoint and cleaner-subagent delegation guards) are untouched. Both satisfied and unsatisfied branches present at lines 589-590.

implementation_notes: The push-gate token gap (cleaner does not write the push-gate token, so bare /push would fail after /clean) is an architectural follow-up item. The /commit then /push chain works around this: /commit's changelog-analyst Phase 10 writes the push-gate token.
Codex Finding 1 (BLOCKER — /commit may bind to wrong close-report): classified out_of_scope. Orchestrator's accepted end-state specifies '/commit then /push' as the correct resolution.
Codex Finding 2 (BLOCKER — 'register the commit' is misleading): classified out_of_scope. Parenthetical matches orchestrator's accepted formulation verbatim.

Codex Finding 3 (MAJOR — AC1 may apply to git diff and git reset lines): classified out_of_scope. QA rejection targeted line 589 only; lines 588/590 were present in iteration 1 and passed QA's AC4 check.

command_enumeration_finding.description: Exhaustive enumeration of all commands/*.md files by ending-workflow adaptation tier. This is deliverable R2 — the answer to the user question 'which other commands also need ending-workflow adaptation'.

tier_A_full_devchain_needed.gap: Should mirror dev.md Step 15: explicit /close → /commit → /push chain with /update --temp
tier_A_full_devchain_needed.rationale: dev-command.md is a dev-series command (full BA→dev→QA pipeline) but its ending workflow is weaker than dev.md. User expects identical behavior.

tier_B_push_only_needed.current_state: Step 17 Next Steps now has /push guidance (this cycle's fix)
tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: FIXED in this cycle — push-gate workaround documented

tier_B_push_only_needed.current_state: Ends with 'Report changes made' — no /commit or /push mention after editing INDEX.md and CLAUDE.md auto-sections
tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: doc-sync modifies files via Edit/Write in orchestrator context; should note that changes may be uncommitted edits needing /push
tier_B_push_only_needed.adaptation: Add conditional note: 'if changes were made, review with git diff, then commit and publish with /push'

tier_B_push_only_needed.current_state: Ends after git commit — no /push mention
tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: Should note that after the commit, /push is the next step to publish to remote
tier_B_push_only_needed.adaptation: Add one-line note: 'After the commit, run /push to publish to remote'

tier_B_push_only_needed.current_state: Creates HTML prototype files; ends with 'Save as {name}.html' — no /commit or /push mention
tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: As a quick-prototyping tool, lowest priority; a note about /commit + /push could be added
tier_B_push_only_needed.adaptation: Add one-line note: 'To version-control: commit the file then /push'

out_of_scope_finding.finding: push-gate token gap in cleaner subagent
out_of_scope_finding.description: The cleaner subagent commits inline without writing the push-gate token that bare /push requires. The /commit then /push chain (documented in clean.md) works around this: /commit's changelog-analyst Phase 10 writes the push-gate token.
out_of_scope_finding.recommended_resolution: Separate dev cycle: have agents/cleaner.md write the canonical push-gate token (same format as changelog-analyst Phase 10, agents/changelog-analyst.md:292-302) after its inline commit succeeds. This would allow bare /push to work after /clean without needing an intermediate /commit.

recommendations: Open a follow-up cycle to have agents/cleaner.md write the push-gate token after its inline commit, enabling bare /push usage after /clean
Consider adding /push guidance to commands/dev-command.md (Tier A — HIGH priority) in a separate cycle
Consider adding /push guidance to commands/doc-sync.md (Tier B — MEDIUM priority) in a separate cycle

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Three findings: (1) BLOCKER — /commit may bind to wrong close-report without /close artifacts; advised /commit --force or cleaner push-gate write. (2) BLOCKER — 'register the commit' phrasing is misleading since /commit creates a new commit. (3) MAJOR — AC1 may apply to git diff and git reset lines in Steps 1 and 3.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1: classified out_of_scope — orchestrator's accepted end-state specifies /commit then /push; --force detail not required. Finding 2: classified out_of_scope — parenthetical matches orchestrator's accepted formulation verbatim. Finding 3: classified out_of_scope — QA rejection targeted line 589 only; lines 588/590 passed QA's AC4 check in iteration 1; expanding scope violates Minimum-Diff Rule.
